content:
4 min read
Just now

--

No hype. No “AI changed my life.” Just 30 days of honest notes.
A month ago, I decided to stop reading articles about AI tools and actually use them — every day, for real work, for 30 days straight. Not a weekend experiment. Not cherry-picked screenshots. Just ChatGPT and Claude, woven into my actual routine, with a notes app open next to me tracking what helped and what didn’t.
This is that log, cleaned up.
Why I Did This
I’d read a dozen “AI changed my life” posts and trusted none of them. They all sounded like ads. So I set myself a simple rule: use both tools daily for a month, write down what actually happened — good, bad, or boring — and publish it even if the conclusion was “meh.”
Here’s what I found.
Week 1: The Novelty Wore Off Fast
The first few days felt like magic. I asked ChatGPT to summarize a dense report, draft an awkward email, and explain a concept I’d been too embarrassed to Google. All three took me under a minute.
But by day four, the novelty was gone, and I started noticing the friction:

• I had to re-explain context every single conversation.

• Generic prompts got generic answers — “write me an email” produced something I still had to rewrite.
